name: Carmel Filled Chocolate Cookies
keywords: best of 50 years of bake off
recipeIngredient: 4 oz vanilla-flavored candy coating, if desired
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift 2 1/2 cups flour into a bowl; level off.
2. In a medium bowl, combine flour, cocoa powder, and baking soda; mix well.
3. In a large bowl, cream together 1 cup sugar and brown sugar with margarine until light and fluffy.
4. Add vanilla extract and eggs; beat well.
5. Gradually add the flour mixture to the sugar mixture; blend well.
6. Stir in half of the chopped pecans.
7. If necessary, cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes for easier handling.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
9. For each cookie, shape about 1 tablespoon of dough around 1 caramelize candy, covering completely.
10. In a small bowl, combine the remaining chopped pecans and 1 tablespoon granulated sugar.
11. Press one side of each ball into pecan mixture; place, nut side up, 2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets.
12. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 7-10 minutes or until set and slightly cracked.
13. Cool cookies for 2 minutes before removing them from the cookie sheets.
14. Transfer to a wire rack; cool completely.
15. Melt candy coating in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until smooth.
16. Drizzle over cooled cookies.
